skill.name: test-driven-development
skill.description: Guides agents through strict test-driven development for features, bug fixes, refactoring, and behavior changes.
skill.summary: Apply red-green-refactor discipline before writing implementation code.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high and medium alerts, but manual review found they are Markdown documentation false positives rather than malware indicators. The real concern is that the skill strongly tells agents to delete code written before tests, which can be destructive without user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Potentially Destructive TDD Enforcement
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to delete implementation code written before a failing test. This supports strict TDD, but it can cause loss of uncommitted user work if followed without confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The deletion instruction is explicit and repeated in the TDD workflow. The surrounding context is legitimate development guidance, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Test Command Execution Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill tells agents to run project test commands such as npm test. This is normal for a TDD skill, but it requires executing commands from the user repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced commands are visible shell examples for running tests. No user input interpolation, persistence, or exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Each Ruby or shell backtick alert was reviewed. The cited text is Markdown code fences, diagrams, illustrative snippets, or normal npm test examples, not bundled executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation examples in SKILL.md and testing-anti-patterns.md. No evidence found for standalone scripts, install hooks, persistence, or command injection.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high and low blocker alerts map to front matter, prose, table entries, and sample test cleanup code. No cryptographic algorithm use, port scan, or network probe is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary documentation text at the cited locations. No evidence found for weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, or network reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Teams often add tests too late and miss behavior gaps. This skill guides agents through red-green-refactor steps before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to write a failing test before implementation.
Checks that the failure reason matches the missing behavior.
Limits implementation to the smallest change that passes tests.
Encourages refactoring only after tests are green.
Provides guidance for avoiding mock-heavy test anti-patterns.
Supplies a completion checklist for test-first work.
limitations: It does not choose a test framework for the project.
It does not generate tests without project context.
It may recommend deleting code written before tests.
It expects the agent to run local test commands.

use_cases.title: Implement a New Feature
use_cases.description: Start from expected behavior, create a failing test, then add only enough code to pass.

use_cases.title: Fix a Reproducible Bug
use_cases.description: Capture the bug in a failing test before changing production logic.

use_cases.title: Refactor Existing Code
use_cases.description: Protect behavior with tests before cleanup and verify that behavior stays unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Begin a TDD Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill for this feature. First identify one behavior, then write the smallest failing test for it.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Bug With a Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use TDD to fix this bug. Write a failing regression test that reproduces the issue before changing implementation code.

prompt_templates.title: Review Test Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing anti-pattern guidance to review these tests for mock assertions, test-only production methods, and incomplete mocks.

prompt_templates.title: Guide a Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use TDD discipline for this refactor. Confirm current behavior with tests, keep changes minimal, and run tests after each cleanup step.

output_examples.input: Add retry behavior to a failed operation.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies retry success as the first behavior, writes a failing test, implements the smallest retry loop, then verifies all tests pass.

output_examples.input: Fix a form that accepts empty email values.
output_examples.output: The agent creates a regression test for empty email rejection, confirms it fails, adds validation, and reruns the test suite.

output_examples.input: Review tests that assert on mock components.
output_examples.output: The agent flags mock-focused assertions and recommends testing visible behavior or using a real component when practical.

best_practices: Confirm each new test fails for the expected reason before implementation.
Keep each test focused on one observable behavior.
Refactor only after the focused test and related tests pass.
anti_patterns: Writing production code first and adding tests afterward.
Testing mock behavior instead of user-visible or system-visible behavior.
Adding production methods only to make tests easier.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides agents through strict test-driven development for features, bug fixes, refactoring, and behavior changes.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It includes examples that tell agents to run project tests. Users should review command execution in sensitive repositories.

faq.question: Why was the risk level set to medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains explicit instructions to delete code written before tests. That can be destructive without confirmation.

faq.question: Were the static high-risk findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. Manual review found Markdown examples and prose, not weak cryptography, reconnaissance, or malicious execution.

faq.question: Can this work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should this skill not be used?
faq.answer: Avoid it for throwaway prototypes, generated code, or configuration-only changes unless a human reviewer requests tests.
